summary: A UC Berkeley faculty profile for Pieter Abbeel, detailing his research bio, startup affiliations (Covariant, Gradescope), and a collection of news clippings about his lab's work in deep reinforcement learning, low-cost robotics, and automation.

principles.statement: Design robotic hardware for the assumption that it will be controlled by intelligent AI, not just rigid programming.
principles.rationale: Future robots will operate much like humans do, relying on AI systems that process visual and force feedback rather than executing purely pre-programmed geometric paths.
principles.source_quote: This robot is designed for the assumption that in the future, robots will be controlled much more intelligently by AI systems that use visual feedback, that use force feedback, much like how humans control their own arms.

signature_quotes.text: This robot is designed for the assumption that in the future, robots will be controlled much more intelligently by AI systems that use visual feedback, that use force feedback, much like how humans control their own arms.
signature_quotes.context: Discussing the design philosophy behind 'Blue', a low-cost, human-friendly robot.

signature_quotes.text: If this happens 50 years from now, there is plenty of time for the educational system to catch up to the job market.
signature_quotes.context: Discussing the long-term impact of AI and machine learning on the future job market and human labor.
